explicit_text: I served in the United States Army during World War II, specifically in the infantry. I was part of the regular Army and spent several years on active duty, including training troops and fighting in the Philippines. The Army was my branch of service, and I learned a lot from my sergeants and officers, especially during my time at Fort Benning. My experience shaped much of my life, and I remain proud of my service.

After the surrender, we were forced to march for days with little food or water, and many died along the way. It was a brutal experience that nearly killed me and thousands of others, and it is something I want everyone to remember and believe happened.

explicit_text: The Disabled American Veterans organization helped me secure my disability status after the war. In 1982, Congress passed a law recognizing all prisoners of war as 100 percent disabled, and the DAV assisted me in getting that status quickly. They continue to support veterans like me with examinations and benefits.
